page: Microsoft Azure D8als_v7 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the Dalsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD processor and is configured with 16.0 GB of system memory. This allocation results in a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU, positioning the instance in a lower-memory tier relative to standard general-purpose configurations.

The instance does not include bundled local storage, but it is premium storage capable, allowing users to attach external persistent storage volumes. It contains no integrated graphics processing units and does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

The primary tradeoff of the D8als_v7 is its constrained memory capacity of 2.0 GB per core and the lack of local storage. However, this resource density provides qualitative cost efficiency for workloads that require dedicated CPU resources but do not demand large memory footprints or local scratch space. It avoids the higher costs associated with memory-heavy or storage-bundled instances.

This hardware profile is suitable for compute-focused workloads with low memory requirements, such as web serving, batch processing, microservices, and distributed compute nodes where memory utilization is predictable and low.
description: Microsoft Azure D8als_v7 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine from the Dalsv7 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is equipped with 8 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD processor and 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. The instance does not include bundled local storage or integrated GPUs, though it is premium storage capable for external disk attachments. This resource configuration offers qualitative cost efficiency by reducing memory overhead expenses for CPU-bound tasks. The D8als_v7 is designed for workloads that require dedicated compute power without large memory footprints, such as web serving, batch processing, and microservices.

tagline: A dedicated eight-core AMD compute instance with a low-memory footprint, designed for cost-efficient CPU-bound workloads and microservices.
